rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or declared in the same annex.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning expression types with declared helper types or using valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool).
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only reference visible AADL features or locally declared helpers to avoid resolution errors.
Use valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for constants and equation declarations to prevent type resolution failures.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and placed in appropriate component scopes.
Guarantees and assumptions in component types should relate visible inputs to visible outputs, not implementation-only paths.
anti_patterns: Do not reference package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model's configured dependencies.
Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes instead.
irrelevant_topics: Component type annexes vs implementation annexes
